DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-01-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANT F.S.A.C., INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT.

F.S.A.C., Inc.'s demurrer to WVN Asso. LLC and Mountain Bay Construction Inc.'s cross-complaint is overruled.

In this construction defects case, general contractor Mountain Bay and developer WVN cross-complained against subcontractors including FSAC which installed fire alarm systems at the property. (Mot. 6:8-9; Terani Decl. para. 3.) FSAC now demurs to the cross-complaint on ground the cross-complaint fails to state facts sufficient to constitute causes of action against it for (1) express indemnity; (2) implied indemnity; (3) equitable indemnity; (4) breach of contract; and (5) negligence.

The cross-complaint alleges FSAC and the other cross-defendant subcontractors "breached the express indemnity provisions?in connection with Plaintiff's claims" (cross-compl. at 15:19-22); "breached their implied covenant to indemnify in connection with Plaintiff's claims" (id. at 16:15-17); "breached their respective contracts" (id. at 17:19-23); and "breached their respective duties of care" (id. at 18:9-12). The cross complaint seeks equitable indemnification in connection with plaintiff's damages. (Id. at 16:26, 17:2). The cross-complaint also alleges FSAC contracted with WVN and Mountain Bay to install fire alarm systems at the property, and it incorporates allegations from the complaint that the fire sprinklers, mechanical systems, and electrical systems were defective. (Cross-compl. at 3:20-22, 8:15-20.)

FSAC argues these allegations do not implicate it as a matter of law: it merely connected the fire alarm system to already-installed plumbing and had nothing to do with the allegedly defective fire sprinklers, mechanical systems, and electrical systems. (Mot. 6:5-13; Reply 2:20-23; Terani Decl. para 3-4.) FSAC's involvement in these allegedly defective systems, however, is a question of fact that cannot be decided on a demurrer. There are sufficient ultimate facts alleged to state causes of action. The demurrer is overruled.

Order is signed.
